race.comment: TRES MAGNIFIQUE (10) won't know herself in this grade given the races she was tested in after the nice debut win. After a luckless fifth at Caulfield on Guineas Day she started $3.90 in a Listed race at Moonee Valley, when again a little stiff, and had probably had enough by the time she got to the Red Roses. She's trialled up like a horse who will contest better races than this later in the prep. Not to the same extent, but STATICE (12) is another down in grade and the draw gives her the chance to take up her favoured on-pace role without doing too much work. MINE ALL MINE (8) is across from SA for the first-up run and a couple of nice recent trials suggest he can play a role from a good draw. EPIC CENTRE (1) was a little plain last start in 66 grade, but the previous two in similar class were efforts that would make him competitive here. THUNDER PARK (5) ran an improved race last start when leading and should be elevated if it's an advantage to be on-speed.
